metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verification step to AC3: explicitly require that each Quickstart command is checked against `rebar --help` / `_cli/help/*.txt` output (e.g. 'cross-reference every shown command with `rebar <cmd> --help` and document discrepancies before merging').
findings.finding: AC3 ('commands shown are copy-pasteable and correct against the current CLI') has no described proving step — no instruction to cross-check each shown command against `rebar --help` or the `_cli/help/*.txt` files, and no CI or manual verification step is specified. An agent could write plausible-looking but subtly wrong commands (wrong flag, renamed subcommand) and the plan provides no mechanism to catch it.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to: 'The commands shown are copy-pasteable and verified correct: each command in the Quickstart must be cross-checked against `rebar --help` and the corresponding `_cli/help/<cmd>.txt` file, with any discrepancy resolved before merge.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan how the Quickstart install line relates to the existing Install section — either the Quickstart links/forwards to it (no duplication), or the existing section is trimmed so the install command lives in exactly one place, honoring the 'single-sourced' intent.
findings.finding: The plan labels itself 'single-sourced' but introduces a new install instruction inside the Quickstart while an existing Install section already lives in the README. The plan does not specify how the two install instructions relate (replace / reference / co-exist), creating a future drift risk between them.
findings.location: ## What — 'Install (one canonical line)' bullet; ## Scope; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: In the Quickstart, replace a standalone install command with a one-line reference such as 'Install rebar (see [Installation](#installation) below), then:' so the authoritative install instruction remains in one place and the Quickstart doesn't duplicate it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure the Quickstart's 'close' step uses the actual CLI command (`rebar transition <id> closed` or `rebar transition <id> in_progress closed`) rather than a non-existent `rebar close` shorthand — verify against `rebar transition --help`.
findings.finding: The plan's Quickstart AC includes a 'close' step in the walkthrough, but rebar has no `rebar close` command. Closing a ticket requires `rebar transition <id> <current> closed` (or `rebar transition <id> closed` with auto-detect). If the Quickstart example literally writes `rebar close <id>`, it will fail, violating AC3 ('commands shown are copy-pasteable and correct against the current CLI').
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('install -> first-ticket -> first-agent-flow walkthrough') and AC3 ('commands shown are copy-pasteable and correct')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Quickstart draft, replace the close step with: `rebar transition <id> closed   # in_progress -> closed` (the auto-detect form), which matches the real CLI as shown in tests/golden/cli_help/transition__help.out line 2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the shorthand 'close' in the plan's command sequence with the actual subcommand `rebar transition <id> in_progress closed` (or its two-arg auto-detect form `rebar transition <id> closed`), and verify the inline Quickstart commands against `rebar --help` before merging.
findings.finding: The plan describes a 'close' step ("ready / claim -> close") as one of the ~6 core commands shown inline, but `rebar close` does not exist as a subcommand. Closing a ticket requires `rebar transition <id> <current_status> closed`. If the Quickstart is written with `rebar close ...` it will fail copy-paste (AC3) and mislead newcomers.
findings.location: ## What (bullet: 'ready / claim -> close') and AC3 ('commands shown are copy-pasteable and correct against the current CLI')
findings.suggested_fix: Use `rebar transition <id> closed` (two-arg form, auto-detects current status) as the close step in the Quickstart; add a note that `transition` is the status-change primitive and point to `rebar transition --help` for the full signature.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's stated help-file count from '40' to '50' (the actual number of per-command `.txt` files in `src/rebar/_cli/help/`), and confirm no other CLI surface details in the plan are based on similarly stale counts.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '`rebar --help` already emits a full, authoritative command index backed by 40 per-command help files', but the actual count of per-command help files in src/rebar/_cli/help/ is 50 (51 files total including overview.txt), not 40. This is a verifiable factual error in the plan's premise.
findings.location: Plan section: ## Why — 'backed by 40 per-command help files'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the Why section: 'rebar --help already emits a full, authoritative command index backed by 50 per-command help files'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one 'if something goes wrong' pointer in the Quickstart (e.g., a brief note listing prerequisites to verify and a link to the relevant README section or `rebar --help`) so the non-happy path is not a dead end for newcomers.
findings.finding: The Quickstart walkthrough covers only the happy path (install → init → create → ready/claim → close with expected output) and gives no guidance on what a newcomer should do when a step fails — e.g., init already run, wrong Python version, missing dependency, command not found — leaving the highest-stakes UX moment (first-time setup failure) unaddressed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'install -> first-ticket -> first-agent-flow walkthrough and expected output' / Scope — 'Keep it short (a screenful)'
findings.suggested_fix: After the final step in the Quickstart, add a one-line callout such as: '> **Trouble?** Check [Requirements](#requirements) and run `rebar --help <command>` for usage details.' This satisfies T6 without expanding the section beyond a screenful.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the Quickstart's command sequence against the current `rebar --help` output (src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt, which lists 54 subcommands, not 40) to ensure no relevant command is omitted or mis-spelled.
findings.finding: The plan's rationale states rebar --help 'emits a full, authoritative command index backed by 40 per-command help files', but the actual count is ~54 files in src/rebar/_cli/help/. While this is a prose-rationale inaccuracy (not an edit-target gap), the misstated count may indicate the plan author is working from an outdated mental model of the CLI surface, which could cause the Quickstart to omit or misname commands added since that count was formed.
findings.location: Plan section: 'Why' — '`rebar --help` already emits a full, authoritative command index backed by 40 per-command help files'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan how the Quickstart install step stays single-sourced: either it links directly to the existing `## Install` section (e.g., 'see [Install](#install) — the one-liner for most users is `pipx install nava-rebar`') rather than restating install instructions inline, or state explicitly which channel is canonical for the golden path and why.
findings.finding: The plan says the Quickstart will include 'Install (one canonical line)' but the existing README already has a full multi-channel Install section (Homebrew, pipx, pip, MCP registry, source). The plan provides no positive rationale for which channel to feature or how the Quickstart's install line will defer to — rather than silently diverge from — the existing Install section, undermining its own 'single-sourced' framing.
findings.suggested_fix: Have the Quickstart install step read as a one-liner followed by a parenthetical link, e.g.: `pipx install nava-rebar  # or see [Install](#install) for all channels` — this surfaces one path without duplicating the Install section.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
